title: OpenClaw Docker health check marks healthy gateway as unhealthy

source.license_note: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #62850.

task.summary: Diagnose why the Docker container becomes unhealthy even though /healthz returns HTTP 200.
task.instructions: Use the incident evidence to confirm the false unhealthy state, localize it to the node-based health check command, and submit a repair conclusion through the ops workflow.
task.acceptance_notes: This is an ops-only synthetic incident derived from a real GitHub issue.

visible_context.issue_text: The official OpenClaw Docker image reports `unhealthy` even while curl to /healthz succeeds from inside the container.
error_logs: HEALTHCHECK CMD node -e fetch(...) exits before the request settles
reproduction_steps: Inspect the health-check evidence and compare the node-based probe to curl.
Decide whether the fault has been detected, localized, and repaired.
visible_context.notes: The expected repair points to the health-check command and startup window, not the gateway server itself.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic incident for a false unhealthy Docker health check derived from a real OpenClaw issue.

constraints.notes: Ops-only smoke scenario. The standalone executor remains synthetic.

notes: Synthetic ops scenario derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #62850.
